title: Solar node custom open source pcb (RAK4630/

messages.author: dbl0
messages.text: I've got this up on github now. RAK Wireless Solar Unify Enclosure (IP67, 150×100×45mm) with a built-in 5V solar panel, custom board fits in the top section, with space for a 3× 18650 battery sled in the lower section, notch for N-Male antenna on top and a vent at the bottom. MPPT/UVLO/Enviro onboard. Multi battery chemistries. Complete weatherproof solar node with ~10,000mAh capacity. https://github.com/mtmesh/solar-mesh-hardware/
messages.content_html: I've got this up on github now. RAK Wireless Solar Unify Enclosure (IP67, 150×100×45mm) with a built-in 5V solar panel, custom board fits in the top section, with space for a 3× 18650 battery sled in the lower section, notch for N-Male antenna on top and a vent at the bottom. MPPT/UVLO/Enviro onboard. Multi battery chemistries. Complete weatherproof solar node with ~10,000mAh capacity. https://github.com/mtmesh/solar-mesh-hardware/

RAK4630 solar board/uvlo/mppt/telemetry (RAK unify solar compatible - mtmesh/solar-mesh-hardware
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: bp-gone
messages.text: I love the attention to detail.
messages.content_html: I love the attention to detail.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: bp-gone
messages.text: The INA3221 is tracking the battery and the solar... in a non-all-in-one, I also track the voltage output of the charging circuit (i.e. it should be 5V). I don't really see that as necessary here.
messages.content_html: The INA3221 is tracking the battery and the solar... in a non-all-in-one, I also track the voltage output of the charging circuit (i.e. it should be 5V). I don't really see that as necessary here.

messages.author: dbl0
messages.text: For the bme280 i added it as I want to know if the case is cooking in the sun, the ambient inside va battery charge performance in the extreme cold, possible moisture ingress. For outdoor id jack into the qwiic with an outdoor bulkhead version as well. I have added it in an isolated area, without any copper pours on any layer around it. In other projects a go to much further lengths to isolate them!
messages.content_html: For the bme280 i added it as I want to know if the case is cooking in the sun, the ambient inside va battery charge performance in the extreme cold, possible moisture ingress. For outdoor id jack into the qwiic with an outdoor bulkhead version as well. I have added it in an isolated area, without any copper pours on any layer around it. In other projects a go to much further lengths to isolate them!
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: bp-gone
messages.text: Will this be completely open sourced? Ki-Cad and complete files so that one could order a complete run from JLC?
messages.content_html: Will this be completely open sourced? Ki-Cad and complete files so that one could order a complete run from JLC?

messages.author: dbl0
messages.text: Everything needed to order a run is on git already with a gnu license. Rev k board is inbound for testing at the moment, maybe wait for the all clear before ordering a complete run!
messages.content_html: Everything needed to order a run is on git already with a gnu license. Rev k board is inbound for testing at the moment, maybe wait for the all clear before ordering a complete run!

messages.author: bp-gone
messages.text: Is there enough there to have boards made and assembled with components by JLC? Last I looked I think I’d have to figure out and compile my own bill of materials. And then order PcBs and put it together myself.
messages.content_html: Is there enough there to have boards made and assembled with components by JLC? Last I looked I think I’d have to figure out and compile my own bill of materials. And then order PcBs and put it together myself.

messages.author: dbl0
messages.text: There's a full BOM, gerbers and whats needed. The zip is there you can literally upload to their site. The only thing with JLC is they carry some "local" versions of parts which you'd need to switch out. they usually share footprints etc. I've focussed on the parts being able to be sourced from digikey/mouser/RS in US/EU, as unless you are doing 50 it is probably a bake at home deal for most people.
messages.content_html: There's a full BOM, gerbers and whats needed. The zip is there you can literally upload to their site. The only thing with JLC is they carry some "local" versions of parts which you'd need to switch out. they usually share footprints etc. I've focussed on the parts being able to be sourced from digikey/mouser/RS in US/EU, as unless you are doing 50 it is probably a bake at home deal for most people.

messages.author: dbl0
messages.text: but I plan to do about 50 units for our mesh once this Rev K passes the testing over the next few weeks. doing a combvined order would be of value for all
messages.content_html: but I plan to do about 50 units for our mesh once this Rev K passes the testing over the next few weeks. doing a combvined order would be of value for all

messages.author: bp-gone
messages.text: The ZIP doesn't have a BOM, you have that at Digikey as referenced in the README. But I wish you well with your project.
messages.content_html: The ZIP doesn't have a BOM, you have that at Digikey as referenced in the README. But I wish you well with your project.

messages.author: dowjames
messages.text: I wish people would just share the design files instead of just gerbers.. It's not open source unless the community is able to freely modify or change it. (edited)
messages.content_html: I wish people would just share the design files instead of just gerbers.. It's not open source unless the community is able to freely modify or change it. (edited)
